quiz.title: Unit 4 Quiz 3, Grade 10 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Cell Biology: Prokaryotic Cell Structure
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Prokaryotic Features, DNA Location, Ribosomes, Comparison with Eukaryotes

questions.question: What ribosome type is found in prokaryotic cytoplasm?
questions.explanation: Prokaryotes have 70S ribosomes. Eukaryotic cytoplasm has 80S ribosomes, though their mitochondria and chloroplasts contain 70S ribosomes.

questions.question: Prokaryotes are classified as belonging to which two domains?
questions.explanation: The three-domain system classifies life into Bacteria, Archaea, and Eukarya. Prokaryotes comprise both Bacteria and Archaea, which are distinct from each other genetically and biochemically. Protists, fungi, plants, and animals are all part of the Eukarya domain.

questions.question: What is the major distinguishing characteristic of fungi?
options: They have cell walls made of chitin
They lack a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles
They move using flagella for locomotion
questions.explanation: Fungi are eukaryotic heterotrophs with cell walls made of chitin. They do not perform photosynthesis (plants do that), they have nuclei and organelles (unlike prokaryotes), and most fungi are non-motile (unlike some protists with flagella).

questions.question: Which of the following best demonstrates the unity among all organisms?
options: The structure and function of DNA
The presence of multicellularity in all life forms
The requirement of oxygen for metabolic processes
questions.explanation: All known life forms use DNA as their genetic material, with the same basic structure (double helix) and the same genetic code, demonstrating common ancestry. Multicellularity is not universal (many organisms are unicellular), not all organisms require oxygen (anaerobes exist), and matching DNA sequences show relatedness but not universal unity.

questions.question: Where is DNA located in prokaryotes?
questions.explanation: Prokaryotic DNA floats freely in the nucleoid region without a membrane enclosure, unlike eukaryotic DNA which is inside a nucleus.

questions.question: Which organisms are classified as prokaryotes?
questions.explanation: All bacteria and archaea are prokaryotic. Plants, animals, fungi, and protists are eukaryotic. Viruses are not cells.

questions.question: What do prokaryotic cells lack?
questions.explanation: Prokaryotes lack membrane-bound organelles like mitochondria, ER, and Golgi. They do have cell membranes, DNA, and ribosomes.

questions.question: Which of the following structures is ALWAYS found in both eukaryotic and prokaryotic cells?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are found in all cells (both prokaryotic and eukaryotic). Nucleus and mitochondria are only in eukaryotes. Cell walls are found in plants, fungi, bacteria, and archaea but not in animal cells, so they are NOT always present in both.

questions.question: Which of the following organisms does not contain a membrane-bound nucleus?
questions.explanation: Streptococcus is a bacterium, which is prokaryotic and lacks a membrane-bound nucleus. Human liver cells, yeast (fungus), and oak leaf cells are all eukaryotic.

questions.question: Which structure is NOT found in a typical prokaryotic cell?
questions.explanation: Prokaryotes lack membrane-bound organelles like mitochondria, ER, and Golgi. They do have a nucleoid with circular DNA, 70S ribosomes, and typically a cell wall.

questions.question: One of the following is absent in a prokaryotic cell.
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are absent in prokaryotes. They do have a plasma membrane, ribosomes (70S), and DNA (circular in the nucleoid region).

questions.question: What is the main genetic material of prokaryotes?
questions.explanation: Prokaryotes typically have a single circular chromosome made of double-stranded DNA. Some also have small circular plasmids. Eukaryotes have linear DNA.

questions.question: What is the region containing DNA in prokaryotes known as?
questions.explanation: The nucleoid is the region in prokaryotes where the circular DNA is located. It is not enclosed by a membrane. Nucleus, nucleoplasm, and chromatin are features of eukaryotic cells.

questions.question: One of the following is not a difference between Prokaryotic and Eukaryotic Cells
questions.explanation: The age of the cell is not a consistent difference between prokaryotes and eukaryotes. Both can be young or old. Presence of a nucleus, cell size, and cellular complexity are key distinguishing features.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have a strong understanding of prokaryotic cell structure.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review the differences in DNA location and ribosome types.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on what prokaryotes lack compared to eukaryotes.
scoreMessages.0: Please review prokaryotic cell structure, DNA organization, and key features.
